calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: MECH-423 cross-model super-additivity 3-arm ablation (supersedes 680c): stabilise integrated co-training + inf/NaN-guarded readiness cosine
items.note: Supersedes V3-EXQ-680c. Per the confirmed failure_autopsy_V3-EXQ-680c_2026-06-15: 680c self-routed substrate_not_ready_requeue because the readiness cosine went NaN -- 4/5 INTEGRATED_PAIR seeds diverged to the world_R2 -1 clamp floor (shared-encoder grads -> inf) despite GRAD_CLIP_NORM=1.0; the one stable seed (789) showed a genuine net-negative shared-encoder cosine (-0.40). MECH-423 NOT weakened (the self-route is the claim-designed non_degenerate response; 680b's 2/3 super-additive seeds stand). TWO coupled fixes: (a) STABILISE the integrated co-training -- separate LOWER encoder LR (ENC_LR=1e-4 vs HEAD_LR=1e-3) via Adam param groups, an encoder-gradient WARMUP (shared latent detached for the first 10 P0 episodes for the integrated arms, then co-train), and a per-step non-finite-loss guard; GRAD_CLIP_NORM=1.0 + R2_SCORE_FLOOR=-1.0 retained. Acceptance precondition: >=4/5 PAIR seeds USABLE (finite world_R2 off the -1 floor AND finite R1 grad/cosine) before any cosine is read. (b) GUARD the readiness cosine -- min_cos over usable seeds only; separate n_diverged_seeds readout. PRE-REGISTERED 3-WAY FORK: FORK 1 diverged-seeds dominate (n_usable<4) -> substrate_not_ready_requeue/co_training_diverged -> diagnose/stronger stabiliser, do NOT iterate blindly; FORK 2 stable + cosine>=0 on usable seeds -> readiness MET -> super-additivity verdict runs (the actual MECH-423 test); FORK 3 stable + FINITE net-negative cosine -> substrate_not_ready_requeue/r1_grad_cosine_net_negative -> /implement-substrate (shared-latent objective reconciliation) or narrow MECH-423, NOT another re-queue. experiment_purpose=evidence (self-routes non_contributory/non_degenerate=False when readiness unmet; weights MECH-423 only when FORK 2 fires). Smoke: validate_experiments --strict OK; --dry-run end-to-end (warmup + non-finite guard + FORK-1 self-route at dry scale + sentinel).

items.title: BASE harm-landscape discriminativeness diagnostic (diagnose-first for the SD-059/MECH-358 bridge retest re-queue)
items.note: Diagnose-first (user-chosen) for the 2026-06-11 V3-EXQ-603o substrate_not_ready_requeue. 603o already ran the 603l-autopsy redesign (headroom+continuous metric) and failed ONLY harm_landscape_discriminative_on_base (harm_eval_range>=0.02 on 1/3 seeds at proximity_harm=0.15) while the continuous bridge lift was already strong (both ~50.3 vs base ~18.65). Claim-free (claim_ids=[], diagnostic): BASE arm only (bridge OFF), 4 cells x 3 seeds, Stage-H only; proximity_harm sweep {0.10 positive-control,0.12,0.15} at harm_lr=1e-3 + a {0.15,harm_lr=3e-3} training-strength rescue cell, keyed on the SAME harm_eval_range>=0.02 statistic. Locates whether the base-harm-landscape failure is regime-difficulty (find hardest trainable proximity_harm) or harm-training-strength (3x LR rescue). Positive-control gate: if 0.10 fails on >=2/3, self-routes substrate_not_ready_requeue (harm-pathway/metric broken), never a regime verdict. Output is the parameter for the corrected SD-059/MECH-358 evidence re-queue V3-EXQ-603q. V3-EXQ-603o is CONSUMED (completed 2026-06-11).
